scope.summary: Implement TackStrength as a keyframeable Track<f32> property animating the force scalar of each named tack constraint over the timeline, enabling progressive stitching/unstitching of trim-to-garment attachments.
acceptance_criteria: Each TackConstraint carries a named tack_id so individual tacks are addressable as animation targets.
Track<TackStrength> is a valid keyframeable target in the property registry, distinct from the closure-t track on MT-288.
The solver reads the interpolated tack strength scalar each substep and scales the tack constraint correction by that value (0.0 = fully unstitched, 1.0 = full tack force).
A timeline authoring an unstitching sequence (TackStrength 1.0 -> 0.0 over N frames) causes the trim to detach from the panel after the keyframe transition.
scope.risk_if_missed: MD-parity for animated tack is undelivered. Trim reveal, armor disassembly, and costume-reveal use cases require per-tack strength animation. Without named tack targets the only closure animation is zipper-slider t, leaving the general trim-to-garment detach case unimplemented.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
